theme_and_archetype.description: A premium, intelligent, and reassuring aesthetic designed for high earners (£50k-£200k+). Eschews modern 'SaaS-bro' gradients and excessive border-radius in favor of crisp edges, classic serif typography, sterile monospace for numbers, and deep heritage colors (forest green, navy, brass/gold).

typography.pairing_strategy: Old Money Tech (Classic Serif Head + Sterile Mono Body/Numbers)

scale.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-5xl tracking-tight font-medium text-primary

rules: Financial figures must use the monospace font for tabular alignment.
Headings should use font-medium or font-semibold, not font-black.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous Spacing, Optical Alignment, Crisp Edges
spacing_and_layout.borders: 1px solid #E5E0D8 (crisp, warm-tinted borders). Border radius max 2px (rounded-sm) for a sharp, tailored look.
spacing_and_layout.wizard_layout: A central, focused column on desktop (max-w-3xl) with ample vertical padding (py-12 to py-24). The wizard steps should feel like high-end print forms.
spacing_and_layout.dashboard_layout: The 'Control Room' grid layout. grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 with gap-6. Density is higher here than the wizard.
spacing_and_layout.containers: All containers holding form inputs must have p-8 or p-12. No cramped inputs.

component_strategies.wizard_stepper: Text-based or subtle line-based progress indicator. Avoid bulky colorful circles.
component_strategies.inputs_and_forms: Shadcn components heavily customized: square corners (rounded-none or rounded-sm), pure white background, 1px warm border. Focus state must use ring-primary (#1E3F20). Number inputs for money should have a persistent '£' prefix.
component_strategies.buttons: Primary buttons: bg-primary text-white, square corners, px-8 py-6. Hover: bg-[#152D16]. Secondary/Outline buttons: border-primary text-primary. No pill-shaped buttons.
component_strategies.toggles: Shadcn Switch customized to use #1E3F20 when active.
component_strategies.charts: Recharts. Use simple bar charts (stacked for salary breakdown) and minimal line charts (compound growth). Remove default grid lines or make them extremely faint (#E5E0D8). Axis text must use monospace font.
component_strategies.print_to_pdf: Place a highly visible, elegant 'Export to PDF' button in the Step 4 Results Dashboard. Utilize a JS library (e.g., html2pdf.js or jspdf+html2canvas) to capture the dashboard node.

motion_and_interactions.transitions: Subtle and slow. transition-all duration-300 ease-in-out.
motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Buttons and interactive cards slightly lift (-translate-y-0.5) with a soft, diffuse shadow (shadow-md).
motion_and_interactions.page_transitions: Wizard steps should fade in softly using Framer Motion (opacity: 0 to 1, y: 10 to 0).

image_urls.description: Low angle view of high-rise buildings in financial district. Use with a heavy white/parchment overlay (bg-white/80 or bg-[#FBFBF9]/90) to create a subtle watermark background for the wizard.
Subtle paper texture. Can be used as a background image with mix-blend-multiply at very low opacity (5-10%) to give the app a 'print' feel.

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure all text, especially muted labels, passes APCA/WCAG AA standards against the parchment background.
accessibility_and_testing.testing_attributes: All inputs, buttons, toggles, and major output values MUST have 'data-testid' attributes (e.g., data-testid='input-gross-salary', data-testid='toggle-pension', data-testid='result-take-home').

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. FRAMEWORK: Use React, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI (customized).
2. WIZARD STATE: Use a robust state management solution (React Context or Zustand) to store inputs across the 4 steps, as Step 4 aggregates all data.

3. CALCULATIONS (CRITICAL): Ensure UK tax bands (England/Wales vs Scotland) are implemented correctly for higher (£50k+) and additional rate (£125k+) taxpayers. Include Personal Allowance tapering above £100k.
4. SALARY SACRIFICE LOGIC: Deduct sacrifice amounts BEFORE applying income tax and NI. For EV, calculate 2% BIK on the car's value and add to taxable income.
5. CHARTS: Use Recharts for Step 4. Ensure tooltips are styled to match the 'Old Money' theme (sharp corners, serif font for headers, mono for values).
6. PDF EXPORT: Add a 'Download Report' button on the final step that triggers html2canvas/jsPDF to export the results view.
7. FONTS: Import 'Cormorant Garamond' (400,500,600), 'IBM Plex Sans' (400,500), and 'IBM Plex Mono' (400,500) from Google Fonts in the HTML template.
8. TAILWIND CONFIG: Update tailwind.config.js to include these specific colors, border-radius overrides, and font-families.
